html:
For specific vegetable seeds, see Crop Seeds.

Seeds are a Food Item commonly left on the ground by Birds. While they can be eaten, Seeds are primarily used for Farming by planting them in a farm plot to grow crops. When planted, Seeds have a chance to grow into Corn, Carrot, Pumpkin, Eggplant, Pomegranate, Durian or Dragon Fruit (see Farming for further information). Birds, Tallbirds, Smallish Tallbirds, Smallbirds, and Pigs can eat Seeds and will pick Seeds up if they find them on the ground. Pigs that eat Seeds don't produce Manure. Seeds can be used to bait Bird Traps.

In , when the temperature is 0 or below (which currently only happens during Winter), Seeds that have been planted will stop growing and cannot be forced to grow via Fertilizer.

Toasted Seeds

Birds attracted to Seeds but not Toasted Seeds.

Toasted Seeds are made by cooking Seeds over a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. Unlike their raw counterpart, they restore 1 point of Health and cannot be planted or fed to birds. They also spoil four times faster than uncooked seeds.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants, Seeds can be gained by destroying Tumbleweeds, even during Winter. Seeds also have an additional chance to grow into Watermelons when planted.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Seeds have an additional chance to grow into Watermelons or Sweet Potatoes.

In Hamlet DLC, Seeds have an additional chance to grow into Watermelons, Sweet Potatoes, Aloe, Asparagus or Radishes. The character Wormwood is able to plant Seeds directly into the ground without using a Farm. Also, Seeds can be bought for 1 Oincs at the Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements in the Pig City.

Seed Chances

When planted in a farm, Seeds have the following chances to grow in to each type of crop:

In Don't Starve:

Item Name Chance Chance
 Chance
 Chance
Carrot 31.6% 28.6% 22.2% 13.3%
Corn 31.6% 28.6% 22.2% 13.3%
Eggplant 10.5% 9.5% 7.4% 4.4%
Pumpkin 10.5% 9.5% 7.4% 4.4%
Dragon Fruit 5.25% 4.76% 3.7% 2.2%
Durian 5.25% 4.76% 3.7% 2.2%
Pomegranate 5.25% 4.76% 3.7% 2.2%
Watermelon - 9.5% 7.4% 4.4%
Sweet Potato - - 22.2% 13.3%
Aloe - - - 13.3%
Asparagus - - - 13.3%
Radish - - - 13.3%

Usage

This method keeps seeds fresh. Only works in Don't Starve.

×2

×2

×6

×2


Tips

  • If fed to a bird in a Birdcage, it will produce fresh Seeds, therefore allowing the player to keep Seeds for as long as they want without rotting (In DST, birds will instead have a 33% chance of giving back Guano).
  • No seeds are dropped by birds in the Winter, but the occasional stale or even spoiled seed left over from last season may be found. Crows and Snowbirds will still eat the Seeds, essentially thinning their numbers.
  • When using Horticulture Abridged to grow farm crops you can give them enough water for their entire lifespan to obtain seeds provided they are in season, allowing players to start farms quickly.

Bugs

  • If the Seeds are dropped on the Walrus Camp (during Spring-Autumn), they will get stuck and prevent the player from picking them up.

Gallery

  • Original HD Seeds icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Cooked Seeds icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

“Each one is a tiny mystery.”
–Wilson, when examining Seeds.
“Farming is boring.”
–Willow, when examining Seeds.
“Too small to eat.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Seeds.
“Life, or at least the promise of it.”
–Wendy, when examining Seeds.
“IT'S LIKE SOURCE CODE FOR PLANTS”
–WX-78, when examining Seeds.
“A handful of unidentifiable seeds.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Seeds.
“Not trees.”
–Woodie, when examining Seeds.
“I suppose these won't plant themselves.”
–Maxwell, when examining Seeds.
“I cannot eat them like this. They're better off in the ground.”
–Wagstaff, when examining Seeds.
“Tiny nature bits.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Seeds.
“A small life trapped within. My sympathies.”
–Webber, when examining Seeds.
“Ugh, do I have to like, plant these?”
–Walani, when examining Seeds.
“You may grow up to be delicious one day.”
–Warly, when examining Seeds.
“Woodlegs ain't no landlubbin' farmer!”
–Woodlegs, when examining Seeds.
“WILBA SHALL PUT IN PLOP FORTHWITH”
–Wilba, when examining Seeds.
“Wants dirt”
–Wormwood, when examining Seeds.
“Seedy little guys.”
–Wheeler, when examining Seeds.
“Some seeds. Not sure what kind.”
–Winona, when examining Seeds.
“Let's give them some soil, before they spoil.”
–Wortox, when examining Seeds.
“Put in dirt, florp!”
–Wurt, when examining Seeds.
“It's nice of the birds to share.”
–Walter, when examining Seeds.
“Future plants, or a present snack.”
–Wanda, when examining Seeds.
Seeds are a Food Item commonly left on the ground by Birds. While they can be eaten, Seeds are primarily used for Farming by planting them in a farm plot to grow crops. When planted, Seeds have a chance to grow into Corn, Carrot, Pumpkin, Eggplant, Pomegranate, Durian or Dragon Fruit (see Farming for further information). Birds, Tallbirds, Smallish Tallbirds, Smallbirds, and Pigs can eat Seeds and will pick Seeds up if they find them on the ground. Pigs that eat Seeds don't produce Manure. Seeds can be used to bait Bird Traps.
In , when the temperature is 0 or below (which currently only happens during Winter), Seeds that have been planted will stop growing and cannot be forced to grow via Fertilizer.

“I cooked all the life out of 'em!”
–Wilson, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“No good for farming now.”
–Willow, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Fire make bigger, can eat now.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“I toasted their life away.”
–Wendy, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“THEY CAN NO LONGER GERMINATE”
–WX-78, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“A toasted embryonic plant treat!”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“We call this "lumberjack surprise".”
–Woodie, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“I broiled the life out of 'em.”
–Maxwell, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“A nice, light digestible snack.”
–Wagstaff, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Tiny nature bits, cööked tö death.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Their growing days are over.”
–Webber, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“They get stuck in my teeth.”
–Walani, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Could use smoked paprika...”
–Warly, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Plant 'em in me belly.”
–Woodlegs, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“WILBA CAN'ST EAT-ETH NOW”
–Wilba, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Mmmm...”
–Wormwood, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Not too late to be used as a projectile.”
–Wheeler, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Anyone wanna see how far I can spit the shells?”
–Winona, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“The seeds have been cooked.”
–Wortox, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“Teeny snacks!”
–Wurt, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“You make do with what you have when you're out in the wilderness.”
–Walter, when examining Toasted Seeds.
“I don't know why I bothered cooking them.”
–Wanda, when examining Toasted Seeds.

Tips[edit | edit source]
If fed to a bird in a Birdcage, it will produce fresh Seeds, therefore allowing the player to keep Seeds for as long as they want without rotting (In DST, birds will instead have a 33% chance of giving back Guano).
No seeds are dropped by birds in the Winter, but the occasional stale or even spoiled seed left over from last season may be found. Crows and Snowbirds will still eat the Seeds, essentially thinning their numbers.
When using Horticulture Abridged to grow farm crops you can give them enough water for their entire lifespan to obtain seeds provided they are in season, allowing players to start farms quickly.

The character [[Wormwood]] is able to plant Seeds directly into the ground without using a Farm. Only works in Don't Starve.}} {{Recipe |item3=Gold Nugget |count3=6 |item2=Papyrus |count2=2 |item1=Seeds |count1=2 |result=Birdcage |item = Gold Nugget |tool = Alchemy Engine}} {{Recipe |item2 = Papyrus |count2 = 2 |item1 = Seeds |count1 = 1 |item3 = Manure |count3 = 1 |result = Applied Horticulture |dlc = Don't Starve |character=Wickerbottom}} =={{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips== * If fed to a bird in a [[Birdcage]], it will produce fresh Seeds, therefore allowing the player to keep Seeds for as long as they want without rotting (In DST, birds will instead have a 33% chance of giving back [[Guano]]). * No seeds are dropped by birds in the Winter, but the occasional stale or even spoiled seed left over from last season may be found. Crows and Snowbirds will still eat the Seeds, essentially thinning their numbers. * When using [[Books|Horticulture Abridged]] to grow farm crops you can give them enough water for their entire lifespan to obtain seeds provided they are in season, allowing players to start farms quickly. =={{pic|32|Mosquito}} Bugs== * If the Seeds are dropped on the [[Walrus Camp]] (during Spring-Autumn), they will get stuck and prevent the player from picking them up. == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == CD seeds.png|Original HD Seeds icon from Bonus Materials from [[CD Don't Starve]].
